meta.title: Conduit: Mobile SSH, Mosh & SFTP terminal for iOS and Android
meta.description: A fast, open-source mobile terminal for SSH, Mosh, and SFTP. Run coding agents, edit in nvim, use hardware security keys, and even boot a local Arch Linux shell on your phone.

hero.title: The terminal your servers deserve, on the phone in your hand.
hero.body: Conduit is a mobile-first workspace for SSH, Mosh, and SFTP. Hardware security keys, tmux, and a real Linux shell, engineered so working from your phone never feels like a compromise.

features.intro: Everything a working engineer reaches for over SSH, reworked for a touch screen without dumbing anything down.

ssh-mosh.title: Sessions that survive the subway
ssh-mosh.body: Full SSH with agent forwarding, plus Mosh for connections that ride out dead zones, IP changes, and sleep. Close the app, hop networks, come back, and your shell is right where you left it.
points: SSH with key, password, and agent forwarding
Mosh roaming over flaky mobile links
Predictive local echo when latency spikes

ai-agents.title: Run coding agents from the train
ai-agents.body: Start Claude Code or Codex on the machine where your repo actually lives, then steer it from your phone. It runs in a real shell over SSH or Mosh, so a subway dead zone never kills the run.
points: Full-fidelity TUIs for Claude Code and Codex
Long runs keep going through network drops
Approve, redirect, and read diffs on the go
ai-agents.alt: Conduit running a coding agent over SSH

fleet.title: Every box you own, one tap away
fleet.body: Save machines with their keys, tags, and per-host settings, then filter, search, and sort them however you think. Sessions open in tabs, so production, your homelab, and that one VPS are all a swipe apart.
points: Saved profiles with tags and search
Sort by last used, name, or drag your own order
Tabbed sessions across every host
fleet.alt: Conduit saved machines list with tags and search

tmux.title: tmux, attached before you type
tmux.body: Flip one switch on a host and every connect attaches to your tmux session, or creates it, in the directory and under the name you chose. Prefix, action, and scrollback keys live right in the key row, so Ctrl-B chords stop being finger yoga on glass.
points: Auto attach or create on connect
Per-host session name and start directory
Prefix, actions, and scrollback in the key row

nvim.title: Edit like you never left your desk
nvim.body: Open nvim over SSH and get your real setup: plugins, LSP, treesitter, all of it. True color and a bundled Nerd Font mean icons and syntax land exactly the way they do on your laptop.
points: True-color rendering with ligatures
Nerd Font glyphs built in
btop and redraw-heavy TUIs stay smooth
nvim.alt: Editing a file in Neovim inside Conduit

key-row.title: The keys your phone keyboard forgot
key-row.body: A configurable row above the keyboard carries Esc, Ctrl, Tab, arrows, and function keys, with latching modifiers and real key repeat. Add your own snippets and control combos, or flip on compose mode and stage a whole line with the native keyboard before it hits the shell.
points: Latching Ctrl and Alt, arrows that repeat
Custom snippets and control-key combos
Compose mode for full IME input

sftp.title: Move files without leaving the app
sftp.body: A built-in SFTP browser sits next to your session. Navigate remote trees, upload, download, rename, and delete. No separate app, no context switch.
points: Browse remote filesystems visually
Upload and download in a tap
Rename and delete in place
sftp.alt: Conduit SFTP file browser navigating a remote server

themes.title: Sixteen colorschemes, zero config
themes.body: Catppuccin, Tokyo Night, Gruvbox, Nord, Dracula and more, each tuned end to end so the terminal, UI chrome, and syntax all agree. Pick one and the whole app follows.
points: 16 hand-tuned palettes
Consistent across terminal and UI
Light and dark for every theme
themes.alt: Conduit theme picker showing multiple colorschemes

local-shell.title: A whole Linux box, no server required
local-shell.body: On Android, Conduit can boot a local Arch Linux userland through proot: pacman, compilers, and your favorite CLI tools running unprivileged on the phone itself. No root, and nothing to connect to.
points: Full Arch Linux with pacman
Unprivileged, no root needed
Works completely offline
local-shell.alt: Conduit running a local Linux shell on device

security.title: Log in with the YubiKey in your pocket
security.body: Conduit speaks FIDO2 over NFC and USB. Tap your hardware key to authenticate. Private keys never leave the device, and nothing phishable ever touches your phone. Enroll several keys on one machine and connect with whichever one you have on you.
security.alt: Conduit enrolling a hardware security key for a saved machine

keys.title: Multiple keys per host
keys.body: Backup keys, work keys, travel keys. Enroll them all, no duplicate entries.

enclave.title: Nothing leaves the phone
enclave.body: Credentials live in the secure enclave. No cloud, no sync, no account.

appLock.title: Lock the whole app
appLock.body: An optional app lock puts device biometrics or your passcode in front of saved machines and keys.

hostKeys.title: Host keys you approve
hostKeys.body: New hosts show their fingerprint for explicit review. You manage the trusted list yourself.

openSource.title: Auditable, reproducible, yours
openSource.body: Conduit is fully open source and published on F-Droid with reproducible builds. No trackers, no analytics, no accounts. The code you can read is the app you run.

stats.source: Open source, from crypto to keyboard
stats.install: Ways to install: App Store, F-Droid, Obtainium

cta.title: Put a real terminal in your pocket
cta.body: Free, open source, and ready on the App Store, F-Droid, and Obtainium.

footer.tagline: A mobile terminal workspace for SSH, Mosh, SFTP, and local shells. Built for people who run real infrastructure from their phone.
